summary: Matched several previously stubbed fighter ft_081B functions, mostly around collision/topN syncing and ground/transN-offset velocity handling. The PR also tightened a header prototype for ft_800851D0 and identified ftCommonData.x6C as a float used to scale ground friction when speed exceeds walk_max_vel.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; replaced multiple placeholder comments with matched C implementations for ft_80083B68, ft_80084F3C, ft_80084FA8, ft_800850E0, ft_80085154, ft_800851D0, and cleaned ft_80085204.
Header prototype update; changed ft_800851D0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ft_800851D0(Fighter_GObj*).
Type refinement; changed ftCommonData.x6C from UNK_T to float.

what_changed: Implemented ft_80083B68 to copy CollData.cur_topn to prev_topn, set cur_topn from Fighter.cur_pos, call mpColl_800477E0, write the resulting topN position back to Fighter.cur_pos, then call ft_80081A00.

Implemented ft_80084F3C and ft_80084FA8 as ground-friction helpers using Fighter.gr_vel, co_attrs.gr_friction, co_attrs.walk_max_vel, and p_ftCommonData->x6C as an over-walk-speed friction multiplier.
Implemented ft_800850E0 to either derive gr_vel from x6A4_transNOffset.z when x594_b0 is set or apply ftCommon_8007C930 friction otherwise, then call ftCommon_8007CB74.
Implemented ft_80085154 to rotate x6A4_transNOffset.y/z into self_vel.x/y using cosf and sinf of lstick_angle and facing_dir.
Implemented ft_800851D0 to set self_vel.y from x6A4_transNOffset.y, with an alternate scale factor from ((f32*) Fighter_804D6520)[5] when x197C is non-null.
Rewrote ft_80085204 to use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and the local name fp instead of reading gobj->user_data into a temp_r3 variable.
Added includes needed by the new implementations, including MSL/math.h, ft/ft_0819.h, ft/fighter.h, and lb/types.h.
Updated the ft_800851D0 declaration from unknown return/params to a concrete void Fighter_GObj* signature.
Changed ftCommonData.x6C from UNK_T to float, matching its use as a friction multiplier.
smart_moves: Promoted ftCommonData.x6C to float only when a concrete use site demonstrated it participates in floating-point friction scaling.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) consistently in newly matched functions, aligning the implementation with nearby fighter code style and avoiding raw user_data access.
Preserved matching-sensitive local structure where visible, such as unused stack locals in ft_80083B68 and ft_80084F3C.

Separated two similar friction functions by their exact downstream behavior: ft_80084F3C applies ftCommon_8007C930 then ftCommon_8007CB74, while ft_80084FA8 forwards the computed friction and facing_dir to ft_80085030.
Used an extern float symbol ft_804D83B8 in ft_80084F3C rather than assuming every zero comparison can be expressed as a literal, suggesting attention to constant/sdata matching.
decomp_lessons: When a common-data field is consumed by floating-point arithmetic, updating the struct field type can be necessary for both correctness and cleaner matching.
Fighter movement helpers often follow a pattern of deriving or damping velocity, then calling ftCommon_8007CB74 to finalize/update state.
Collision/topN sync code may need to mirror the precise order of CollData.prev_topn, CollData.cur_topn, mpColl processing, and Fighter.cur_pos writeback.
Similar-looking helper functions can differ in important call structure; matching should preserve whether friction is applied directly, forwarded to another helper, or combined with facing_dir.
Small cleanups from raw gobj->user_data access to GET_FIGHTER can be part of a matching PR when they do not disturb generated code.
naming_conventions: Uses address-derived function names in the ft_8008xxxx style for unmatched or partially named fighter functions.
Uses Fighter_GObj* gobj as the standard object parameter type for these fighter helpers.
Uses fp as the local Fighter* variable obtained from GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Uses co for ftCo_DatAttrs* pointing at fp->co_attrs.
Keeps currently unknown fields named by offset/semantic hints, such as x594_b0, x6A4_transNOffset, x197C, and x70_someLStickYMax.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced unused stack locals such as u32 unused[4] and u32 unused, likely to preserve stack layout or instruction scheduling for matching.
Used MSL cosf and sinf for joystick-angle rotation in ft_80085154, matching the expected math library calls rather than manually approximating rotation.
Used ft_804D83B8 as an extern f32 comparison constant in ft_80084F3C, while ft_80084FA8 compares against 0.0F, preserving distinct constant-generation behavior.
Accessed Fighter_804D6520 through a float pointer cast and index 5 in ft_800851D0, matching the observed data access without over-structuring an unknown global.
Kept arithmetic order explicit in ft_80085154: self_vel.x = z_component * cos - y_component * sin; self_vel.y = z_component * sin + y_component * cos.

review_feedback.detail: The provided PR slice includes no human review comments or review body text. No reviewer-requested changes can be inferred from the available evidence.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type ftCommonData.x6C.
Find later work around ft_80084F3C, ft_80084FA8, and ft_80085030 to understand the full ground-friction helper family.
Search for other uses of x6A4_transNOffset and x594_b0 to clarify transN-offset velocity behavior.
Search for Fighter_804D6520 indexing patterns to replace the raw ((f32*) Fighter_804D6520)[5] access with a typed symbol if evidence appears.
Look for follow-up PRs that name ft_80083B68 or document the CollData topN synchronization flow.
